name: Jalan Alor Night Food Street
blurb: KL's most famous open-air street food strip. Plastic stools, smoky woks, and dozens of stalls running until late — chicken wings, satay, char kway teow, durian.
tips: Wong Ah Wah for legendary BBQ chicken wings. Cash speeds things up. Avoid touts at the entrance; walk past for better-priced stalls deeper in.
getting_there: Monorail Bukit Bintang, then 5 min walk through the Changkat area.

name: Lot 10 Hutong Food Court
blurb: Basement food court that hand-picked legendary hawker stalls from across Malaysia — many third-generation, including the famous Soong Kee beef noodles.
tips: Go for an early lunch (11.30am) before the office crowd. Hokkien mee at Kim Lian Kee is the move. Aircon — perfect midday refuge.
getting_there: Monorail Bukit Bintang, Lot 10 mall basement (LG floor).

name: Petronas Twin Towers & KLCC Park
blurb: The iconic 452m twin towers with a Skybridge between them. KLCC Park below has a free playground, splash pad, and the best photo angle of the towers.
tips: Skybridge tickets sell out — book online days ahead. Symphony Lake fountain show: 8pm, 9pm, 10pm. Splash pad opens 10am, kids go wild.
getting_there: LRT KLCC, direct connection to Suria KLCC mall and the towers.

name: Batu Caves
blurb: Massive limestone cave temple complex with the iconic 42m gold Murugan statue and 272 rainbow steps. Macaques everywhere — entertaining and cheeky.
tips: Go before 9am to beat the heat and crowds. Cover knees and shoulders. Don't bring visible food or sunglasses (monkeys will grab them).
getting_there: KTM Komuter from KL Sentral direct to Batu Caves station — 30 min, very cheap.

name: Central Market (Pasar Seni)
blurb: Art deco heritage building turned craft and souvenir bazaar. Batik, songket, pewter, and a quieter Annexe gallery upstairs. Air-conditioned and toddler-friendly.
tips: Prices are flexible — polite haggling is expected. The Kasturi Walk lane outside has cheap eats and street performers in the evening.
getting_there: LRT Pasar Seni, exit directly into the building.

name: Madras Lane Hawker Stalls
blurb: Tucked-away alley behind Petaling Street with iconic stalls: yong tau foo, curry mee, and the legendary chee cheong fun. Pure old-school KL.
tips: Go before 11am — many stalls sell out by lunch. Cash only. The curry mee with cockles is the signature; not for the squeamish but worth it.
getting_there: LRT Pasar Seni, walk through Petaling Street, look for the side alley near Hong Leong Bank.

name: Petaling Street (Chinatown)
blurb: Covered market street under a green roof — knockoff goods, dried foods, herbal medicine shops, and excellent street food. Atmospheric and chaotic.
tips: Famous for fake luxury goods — quality varies wildly. Don't buy the first price quoted. Air Mata Kuching (longan iced drink) at the top of the street is a must.
getting_there: LRT Pasar Seni, 5 min walk.

name: Sunway Lagoon Theme Park
blurb: Six themed parks in one: Water Park, Amusement Park, Wildlife Park, Extreme Park, Scream Park, and Nickelodeon Lost Lagoon. World's largest man-made surf beach.
tips: All-day pass is the way. Bring waterproof phone bag — ride lockers cost extra. Sunway Pyramid mall next door has good food when you're done.
getting_there: BRT Sunway Line to USJ 7, then short walk. Or Grab — about 30 min from KLCC.

name: Imbi Market (ICC Pudu)
blurb: Locals' breakfast institution. Wet market with a hawker section serving banh xeo, pork noodles, sui kow, and Sister Ho's nostalgic Hainanese chicken rice.
tips: Get there by 8am — best stalls close by 11am. Bubur cha cha and ondeh ondeh from Ah Weng Koh's stall are legendary.
getting_there: Monorail Imbi station, 5 min walk to ICC Pudu building.

name: KL Bird Park
blurb: World's largest free-flight walk-in aviary, set in the green Lake Gardens area. Hornbills, peacocks, and flamingos wandering the paths. Stroller-friendly.
tips: Bird feeding shows at 12.30pm and 3.30pm. Go in the morning before it gets blazing. Combine with the Butterfly Park nearby for a full half-day.
getting_there: Grab from KL Sentral (~10 min). KTM Kuala Lumpur station also walkable.

name: Kampung Baru Nasi Lemak Antarabangsa
blurb: Legendary roadside nasi lemak joint open practically 24 hours, in the last Malay village in central KL. Wooden houses next to glass towers — surreal vibe.
tips: Pair the nasi lemak with rendang or sambal sotong. Late-night here is iconic. Walk Kampung Baru's streets after — the kampung-vs-skyline contrast is unique.
getting_there: LRT Kampung Baru station, 5 min walk down Jalan Raja Muda Musa.

name: Restoran Yut Kee (Hainanese Kopitiam)
blurb: Operating since 1928. Old-school Hainanese chicken chop, roti babi, kaya toast, and marble cake. A living slice of pre-war KL.
tips: Closed Mondays. Go for breakfast or early lunch — they sell out. Cash only. Order roti babi if available; rare and excellent.
getting_there: Monorail Medan Tuanku, 5 min walk to Jalan Kamunting.

name: Thean Hou Temple
blurb: Six-tier hilltop Chinese temple with sweeping KL views, lantern-lined courtyards, and dragon-pillared halls. Particularly stunning during Chinese New Year.
tips: Free entry. Sunset light is gorgeous for photos. Quiet on weekday mornings, packed on weekends with wedding photoshoots.
getting_there: Grab from KL Sentral (~10 min) — public transport access is awkward.

name: Putrajaya Day Trip
blurb: Malaysia's planned administrative capital, 30km south of KL. Pink mosque on the lake, futuristic bridges, manicured boulevards, and lake cruises.
tips: Putra Mosque allows visitors outside prayer times — robes provided. Lake cruise is cheap and a great way to see all the bridges. Cool early morning visit.
getting_there: KLIA Transit train from KL Sentral to Putrajaya & Cyberjaya station, then Grab.

name: Kepong Wet Market Breakfast
blurb: Suburban morning market beloved by locals. Real-deal Hong Kong-style dim sum at Mun Wah, plus chee cheong fun stalls, fresh soy milk, and fried fritters.
tips: Get there by 7-8am for peak freshness. Mun Wah dim sum: order the har gow and char siu bao. Walking through the wet market itself is half the experience.
getting_there: MRT Kepong Sentral, then short Grab to Jalan 2/62A.

name: Aquaria KLCC
blurb: 90m underwater tunnel with sharks, stingrays, and giant groupers passing overhead. Smaller tanks with otters, seahorses, and a piranha pool. Solid rainy-day option.
tips: Go right at opening (10am) to walk the tunnel without crowds. Combo tickets with KL Tower if doing both. Allow 1.5 hours.
getting_there: LRT KLCC, in the basement of the KL Convention Centre.

name: Masjid Jamek & Merdeka Square
blurb: Moorish-style mosque at the river confluence where KL was founded, plus the colonial-era Merdeka Square nearby (where Malaysia's independence was declared).
tips: Visit the mosque outside prayer times; modest dress provided free. The River of Life walk along the Klang river is pleasant in the evening.
getting_there: LRT Masjid Jamek, exit directly at the mosque.

name: Genting Highlands
blurb: Mountain casino-and-theme-park resort at 1,800m. Cool 18°C air, an outdoor theme park, indoor SkyTropolis park, and the world's longest cable car ride up.
tips: Bring a jacket — it's genuinely cold. Cable car (Awana Skyway) is the experience. Buy theme park tickets online for big discounts. Expect crowds on weekends.
getting_there: Bus from KL Sentral (1 hr) to Awana Skyway, then cable car to the top.

name: Restoran Sek Yuen (1948)
blurb: Cantonese institution unchanged since the 1940s — wood-fired wok, ancient kitchen, and signature dishes like pei pa duck and yin yong egg.
tips: Closed Tuesdays. Reserve ahead — small space, big reputation. The pei pa duck takes 30 min — order on arrival. Cash preferred.
getting_there: Grab to Jalan Pudu — short ride from Bukit Bintang.

name: KL Tower (Menara KL) & Forest Reserve
blurb: 421m tower on a hill in a 100-year-old protected rainforest right in the city. Sky deck, observation deck, and a free canopy walkway in the forest below.
tips: Sky Deck (open-air glass box) costs more than the standard observation deck and is worth it. Bukit Nanas Forest Reserve walk is free — short but lovely.
getting_there: Monorail Bukit Nanas, then short walk uphill or shuttle bus from the entrance.

name: SS2 Pasar Malam (Monday Night Market)
blurb: Sprawling Monday-night market in Petaling Jaya — fresh fruit, durian, fried snacks, apam balik, otak-otak, popiah. Locals' weekly ritual.
tips: Goes 5pm to ~10pm. Bring small bills. The apam balik (peanut pancake) and freshly fried popiah are essentials. Parking is hellish; Grab in.
getting_there: Grab to SS2, Petaling Jaya — about 30 min from central KL.

name: Jalan Bangkung (Bangsar Hipster Strip)
blurb: Trendy Bangsar street with quality cafes, gelato, izakayas, and craft cocktail bars. Slower, leafier vibe than Bukit Bintang. Good for a chill evening.
tips: Mid-morning weekends are perfect for cafe-hopping with kids — most have outdoor seating. Pulp by Papa Palheta for coffee, Inside Scoop for ice cream.
getting_there: Grab from city center (~15 min). LRT Bangsar is a 10-min walk away.

name: FRIM (Forest Research Institute) Canopy Walk
blurb: 200-hectare rainforest reserve 30 min from KL, with a 200m-long canopy walkway 30m above the forest floor. Real jungle without leaving the city.
tips: Canopy walk closes Mondays and during rain. Get there by 9am — slots fill up. Wear proper shoes; trail to canopy is uphill.
getting_there: Grab to FRIM Kepong — public transport not practical.

name: Restoran Win Heng Seng (Pork Noodles)
blurb: Coffee shop near KLCC famous for one thing: char siew rice and the legendary Imbi-style pork noodles with raw egg stirred in.
tips: Closed Tuesdays. Get there before 11am — they routinely sell out. The char siew is dark, sticky, and excellent. Cash only.
getting_there: Monorail Imbi, 5 min walk to Jalan Yew/Pudu area.

name: Islamic Arts Museum
blurb: One of Southeast Asia's best museums — calligraphy, textiles, Quran manuscripts, and a stunning architecture gallery with miniature mosques.
tips: Underrated. Aircon, beautifully designed, never crowded. Cafe inside is excellent for lunch. Free shoulder shawls if needed.
getting_there: Grab from KL Sentral or KTM Kuala Lumpur. Adjacent to the National Mosque.

name: Devi's Corner (Bangsar Banana Leaf)
blurb: 24-hour Indian-Muslim mamak with the city's best banana leaf rice — fluffy rice, rotating veg sides, and crispy fried chicken or fish curry.
tips: The 'set' banana leaf is the move — they keep refilling sides. Roti canai for breakfast or post-midnight is iconic. Open all hours.
getting_there: Grab to Bangsar — about 15 min from KLCC.

name: TRX (Tun Razak Exchange) Park & Rooftop
blurb: KL's newest skyscraper district with a 10-acre rooftop park atop The Exchange TRX mall. Skyline views, kids' play areas, and dining without entry fee.
tips: Free access via mall lifts. Best at sunset. The mall itself has flagship F&B that's actually good. Air-conditioned escape route on hot days.
getting_there: MRT Tun Razak Exchange, direct connection to the mall.

name: Chow Kit Wet Market
blurb: KL's largest wet market — gritty, real, and unfiltered. Live seafood, exotic fruits, halal meats, and a hawker section serving Malay breakfast classics.
tips: Best 7-10am. The Bazaar Baru stretches across multiple buildings. Try nasi lemak bungkus and kuih from the morning stalls. Wear shoes you don't mind getting wet.
getting_there: Monorail Chow Kit, 5 min walk.

name: Jalan Alor Durian Stalls
blurb: Same street as the night food market, but mid-afternoon onward you'll find a row of dedicated durian stalls — Musang King, D24, XO, all the premium varieties.
tips: Prices vary wildly — get a quote per kg before pointing. Eat on-site (most hotels ban durian). Pair with mangosteen, sold at the same stalls.
getting_there: Monorail Bukit Bintang, 5 min walk.

name: Perdana Botanical Gardens
blurb: Sprawling green park in the historic Lake Gardens area. Free playgrounds, paddle boats on the lake, deer enclosure, and shaded paths. Locals' weekend escape.
tips: Free entry. Stroller-friendly main paths. Combine with Bird Park, Butterfly Park, or Islamic Arts Museum nearby for a full day.
getting_there: KTM Kuala Lumpur or Grab from KL Sentral. Multiple entry gates.
